faq.q1: What exactly does Seogard monitor and audit?
faq.a1: Seogard continuously monitors {count} SEO and GEO rules on every page: meta title and description, canonical, accidental noindex, status codes (404, 500, redirects), SSR/CSR regressions, hreflang, Open Graph, llms.txt, blocked AI citation crawlers (OAI-SearchBot, PerplexityBot, Claude-SearchBot) and much more. Every regression triggers a real-time email alert, ranked by severity. Beyond regression monitoring, Seogard runs a technical audit on every crawl (title length, image alt text, internal linking, structured data, llms.txt, FAQ schema...) consolidated into an SEO health report.
faq.a2: The self-hosted version is free, under the BSL 1.1 license (source available), with no page limit. You install it on your own server with Docker. The Cloud version is usage-based, for teams that prefer not to run the infrastructure.
faq.q3: How is Seogard different from a classic crawler?
faq.a3: Classic crawlers (Screaming Frog, Sitebulb) analyze either the raw HTML or the JavaScript render, but not both at once. Seogard automatically compares both versions of every page to catch invisible regressions, like broken SSR that the browser hides.
faq.a4: Seogard crawls on three triggers: on every deploy (via the CI/CD webhook), on a schedule (daily to monthly, configurable per zone) and on demand. As soon as a regression is detected, an alert with the exact before/after diff is sent by email, with the affected page, the rule that fired and the severity level.
faq.q5: How do I plug Seogard into a CI/CD pipeline?
faq.a5: Seogard ships a native SEO deployment gate. A POST webhook triggers a crawl of the zone on every deploy, and a GET endpoint returns a pass/fail verdict. Three strictness levels (strict, standard, relaxed) let you BLOCK a production deploy that introduces an SEO/SSR regression. Unlike generic synthetic checks (Checkly-style) where you write and maintain your own scripts, Seogard natively applies {count} SEO/GEO rules with zero scripts to write.
faq.q6: Can Seogard block a deploy that breaks SEO?
faq.a6: Yes. Through the CI/CD webhook, Seogard crawls the zone on every deploy and returns a pass/fail verdict. In strict mode, any critical regression blocks the production deploy; standard and relaxed modes adjust the threshold. It is an SEO-native deployment gate: no scripts to write, unlike generic synthetic monitoring tools.
faq.q7: How often does Seogard crawl my site?
faq.a7: Three triggers you can combine: on every deploy (CI/CD webhook), on a schedule (daily, weekly, twice-monthly or monthly, configurable per zone) and on demand from the dashboard. You never wait for a scheduled crawl to catch a post-deploy regression: the webhook catches it at deploy time.
faq.q8: Can I apply different rules and crawl frequencies per site section?
faq.a8: Yes, with zones. A zone groups pages by URL pattern (e.g. /blog, /products, /checkout) and has its own configuration: SEO/GEO rules on or off, scheduled crawl frequency, CI/CD gate strictness, notifications and team access. The deploy webhook can crawl a single targeted zone, for faster CI feedback. You monitor your critical pages (checkout, categories) more strictly than the rest of the site.
faq.q9: How many pages can I monitor?
faq.a9: Self-hosted, there is no limit: it only depends on your server resources. On Cloud, you only pay for pages actually crawled during the month — re-crawling the same pages costs nothing extra. No commitment, no minimum.
faq.a10: Yes. Seogard-Bot respects robots.txt directives and never overloads your servers. It identifies itself with the User-Agent "Seogard-Bot/1.0" and uses a single fixed IP address to make whitelisting easy.
faq.a11: Email, with the before/after diff for every alert. The CI/CD webhook also lets you connect Seogard to any deployment pipeline.

compare.eyebrow: The blind spot of other tools
compare.title:
Your page looks great in the browser.
But what does Google read?
compare.renderedVerdict: Everything is there — in the browser only.
compare.note: That is exactly the window where broken SSR gets indexed as empty HTML. Oncrawl, Lumar, ContentKing crawl without continuously comparing the two. Seogard does.

steps.title: How it works
steps.step1Text: No install, no configuration. Just your site's address.
steps.step2Text: Seogard reads your sitemap, fetches the raw HTML and the JavaScript render of every page, then compares them.
steps.step3Text: SEO/GEO gaps and errors land on your dashboard, sorted by severity, with the steps to fix them.

checks.title: What the scan detects
checks.check1Text: Metas, H1, canonical or content present in the browser but missing from the indexed raw HTML.
checks.check2Text: Title, meta description, Open Graph missing, empty or changed without you knowing.
checks.check3Text: Accidental noindex, broken canonicals, robots.txt blocking Googlebot.
checks.check4Text: 404, 500, soft 404s, redirects to the homepage, redirect chains.
checks.check5Text: JSON-LD missing, invalid, or injected by JavaScript only.
checks.check6Text: llms.txt, blocked AI citation crawlers, the signals ChatGPT and Perplexity rely on.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.a1: Yes. The scan starts a 14-day trial, no credit card. You can also install Seogard self-hosted, free forever (source available, BSL 1.1).
faq.q2: How is this different from Screaming Frog, Oncrawl or Lumar?
faq.a2: Those tools crawl one version of the page. Seogard continuously compares the raw HTML (what Google and AI read) with the JavaScript render (what you see) — the only way to catch broken SSR before it gets indexed as empty HTML.
faq.q3: How long until I get results?
faq.a3: The crawl starts as soon as you sign up. The first pages and alerts show up within seconds; the full scan follows depending on site size.
faq.q4: Do I need to own the site?
faq.a4: You must own the site or have permission to crawl it. The scan respects your robots.txt.
faq.q5: What if my site is behind a firewall / anti-bot protection?
faq.a5: Some protections (strict Cloudflare, BitNinja...) can block our crawler. In that case, just allow our IP — the steps are shown on your dashboard.

identification.heading: Identification
identification.text: Seogard-Bot analyzes your pages to detect SEO regressions (missing metas, broken SSR, incorrect redirects). It respects robots.txt directives and never overloads your servers.

whitelisting.heading: Whitelisting guides
whitelisting.intro: If your firewall (WAF) blocks our crawler, allow our User-Agent or our IP. Pick your WAF below:

cloudflare.step1Title: Step 1 — Create a Custom WAF rule (required)
cloudflare.step1Alt: Important: use or (not and) between the IP and the User-Agent so the rule matches when either one is present.
cloudflare.step2Title: Step 2 — Bot Fight Mode (Free plan only)
cloudflare.step2Intro: On Cloudflare's Free plan, the Skip rule cannot bypass Bot Fight Mode. If the crawl is still blocked after step 1:
cloudflare.step2Alt: Pro, Business and Enterprise plans: no need to turn off Bot Fight Mode. The Skip rule from step 1 bypasses it automatically.

akamai.alt: Alternative: in Bot Manager, add Seogard-Bot to the list of allowed bots.

aws.li3: Add an Allow rule in your Web ACL referencing that IP Set
aws.li4: Place that rule before your blocking rules

other.title: Other firewall
other.method1Text: Add 142.132.133.166 to your firewall's allow list.
other.method2Text: Create a rule that allows requests whose User-Agent header contains Seogard-Bot.

selfHosted.heading: Self-hosted
selfHosted.text: If you host Seogard yourself, the crawler runs on your server. The IP address to whitelist is your machine's, not 142.132.133.166.
selfHosted.step2Title: Use that IP in the guides above
selfHosted.step2Text: Replace 142.132.133.166 with the IP returned in step 1 in the WAF rules of your choice.
selfHosted.step3Text: Seogard-Bot/1.0 is identical on Cloud and self-hosted. You can also whitelist by User-Agent alone.

help.heading: Need help?

help.text2: We can walk you through it step by step.

hero.title: Technical SEO & GEO courses: make your site visible to Google and to AI
hero.subtitle: Short, hands-on courses to understand what Google and AI engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews) actually see of your site — and to fix SEO regressions before they cost you traffic. No dev skills needed: you check with our free tools, you fix with the AI export.

formations.intro: SEO is no longer only about Google. Answers from ChatGPT, Perplexity and AI Overviews read the raw HTML of your pages — not the JavaScript render you see in your browser. A modern site (misconfigured SSR/CSR, structured data injected via JavaScript, a robots.txt that blocks AI crawlers) can go invisible without anyone noticing. These courses teach you to catch it and fix it.

program.title: The first course
program.intro: A complete course in {count} modules, released one at a time. Each module = one concrete problem, a demo on a real site, and the method to check then fix your own. Free — the first modules are coming soon.

course.title: Your site and AI
course.desc: Make your site visible and citable by ChatGPT, Perplexity and Google's AI Overviews. From diagnosis ("can AI see my site?") to the fix — no jargon, no coding required.

final.title: Check your site while you learn
final.desc: Seogard continuously monitors your site for SEO and GEO regressions and alerts you before Google does. 14-day trial, no credit card.

lessons.title: Your site is invisible to AI: the 30-second test
lessons.desc: Start with the diagnosis: in 30 seconds, check what AI actually reads from your pages (the raw HTML, not your browser).

lessons.title: The 5 reasons AI doesn't cite you
lessons.desc: The big picture: the 5 causes that make a site invisible to AI, and where to start.

lessons.title: You're blocking ChatGPT and Perplexity without knowing it (1 line in robots.txt)
lessons.desc: The difference between training and citation, and the robots.txt line that makes you invisible in AI answers.

lessons.title: llms.txt: the file that gets you cited… or pure hype?
lessons.desc: An honest teardown of a trendy file: what it does, what it doesn't, and when it's worth your time.

lessons.title: AI cites your competitors, not you: the real reason (author, date, sources)
lessons.desc: The citation signals that decide who AI features — and how to add them to your pages.

lessons.title: Write your article so AI copies it: the first 30% decide everything
lessons.desc: The citable structure: answer up front, clear headings, questions and answers. What makes your content get copied.

lessons.title: Where to find the questions that get you cited by AI (without making them up)
lessons.desc: Where to gather your audience's real questions for FAQs that AI cites — without inventing any.

lessons.title: Your JavaScript hides your structured data from AI (and your links from Google)
lessons.desc: Why JSON-LD or links injected via JavaScript stay invisible, and how to serve them in the raw HTML.

lessons.title: Redesign or update: the day you go invisible to AI without noticing
lessons.desc: How a deploy silently breaks your AI visibility, and how to catch it before it's too late.

benefits.title: Open to everyone
benefits.desc: Even without coding. You check with our free tools and fix by pasting Seogard's AI export into ChatGPT, Claude or your CMS assistant.

benefits.title: On real sites
benefits.desc: No theory: we open real pages, prove the flaw on screen, show the fix. You leave with a repeatable method.

benefits.title: SEO + GEO together
benefits.desc: Google visibility and visibility in AI answers (ChatGPT, Perplexity, AI Overviews) handled together, because both depend on your raw HTML.

benefits.title: Honest, no magic promises
benefits.desc: No fake traffic or ranking promises. We give you the real mechanics; you decide what to prioritize.
